events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a missing box extension on a deep finish - meaning the electrical box is recessed behind the finished wall surface. This is a code violation. Boxes must be set flush with or extend beyond the finished surface. A box extension/ring is needed to bring the box flush with the finished wall.

This is a fail condition - non-compliant and potentially a fire/shock hazard if wiring is accessible behind the wall surface.

    "Electrical box is recessed behind deep finished wall surface with no box extension/raised cover installed — wiring and live parts are not flush with or extending beyond the finished surface",
    "Exposed wiring connections may be accessible behind wall finish, creating arc/shock and fire hazard"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install an approved box extension or raised plaster cover to bring the box flush with or projecting beyond the finished wall surface before closing out the wall",
    "Verify box fill capacity is not exceeded after adding the extension per NEC 314.16",
    "Re-inspect after correction before wall close-out"
  ],
  "rationale": "A box recessed behind a deep finish without an extension leaves conductors and connections not properly enclosed at the wall plane. NEC requires boxes to be set flush with or extend beyond the finished surface; a missing extension on a deep finish is a non-compliant, defective installation that must be corrected before close-out.",
